phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. Here's what I did and produced.

## Outcome

**Story #9 created and `agent::ready`** — [Paginate the receivables report and standardize the default page size](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-21-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-54c0f9fe/issues/9), labels `type::story`, `agent::ready`.

I ran the full `/plan` ceremony non-interactively (auto-proceeding the HITL gates under `--yes`):
1. **Interrogate** — `plan-context.js` built the envelope. Notable: the sandbox baseline has **no application source** (empty tier snapshot), so the "other list endpoints" and `/reports/receivables` the request references aren't present in this tree.
2. **Author** — one cohesive Story (default-single policy: pagination + the shared default-page-size bump + the supporting index all ride the same pagination surface — near-zero seam between them). Body generated through the canonical `serialize()` to guarantee a parseable shape.
3. **Critics** — both `consolidation` and `pre-mortem` returned `dispatch: false`; text-hygiene clean. Proceeded straight to persist.
4. **Persist** — dry-run pre-pass passed (exit 0), then the real persist created the Story and flipped it ready.

## What the Story pins down (binding acceptance)
- `GET /reports/receivables` takes `page`/`pageSize` and returns `items`/`total`/`page`/`pageSize` matching the peer list endpoints.
- Page-walk covers the full set once (no overlap/omission); `total` is the unpaginated count.
- `totalOutstandingCents` stays a response field = **grand total across all rows**, invariant across pages — never a per-page subtotal.
- **API-wide default page size = 25** via one shared constant (changed from 20), applying to every paginated endpoint.
- A migration adds an index; against ≥5,000 seeded orders the endpoint responds **< 2000ms**.

Verify covers all four via `node --test` contract/e2e tests plus `npm run typecheck`.

## One thing worth flagging
Because this baseline is empty, the advisory `changes[]` for `src/reports/receivables.js` and `src/lib/pagination.js` were auto-normalized from `refactors-existing`→`creates` (non-gating warning). I kept the `refactors-existing` intent deliberately: `changes[]` is an advisory sketch, and the Story's `## Spec` instructs the delivering agent to first **locate** the real API surface (shared pagination helper, receivables handler, orders persistence + migrations) and refactor in place, creating only what's genuinely absent. Acceptance criteria assert observable behavior, not file paths, so they hold regardless of the actual layout the delivery run finds.

Per instructions I did **not** deliver — next step (separate session) would be `/deliver 9`.

The squash commit `d6ea1e3` is on remote `main` with all 17 application files. Delivery is complete.

## Delivery summary — Story #9 landed ✅

**`/deliver 9 --yes`** discovered a single `type::story` (v2 is Story-only; no Epic issue exists) and drove it end-to-end through the one delivery engine.

**Result:** PR [#10](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-21-brownfield-longitudinal-mandrel-54c0f9fe/pull/10) squash-merged to `main` as `d6ea1e3`; Issue #9 CLOSED at `agent::done`. Terminal status: **`landed`**.

### What shipped
The sandbox baseline carried no application source (only the framework overlay), so — exactly as the Story's `## Spec` directed for a genuinely-absent surface — I built the described API to the contract, dependency-free (Node built-in `node:sqlite`, `node:test`, `node:http`):

- **`src/lib/pagination.js`** — the single shared pagination helper. `DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE = 25` is the one constant every list endpoint reads (**AC-4**).
- **`src/reports/receivables.js`** — `GET /reports/receivables` paginated on the shared `page`/`pageSize` → `items`/`total`/`page`/`pageSize` envelope (**AC-1/AC-2**), with `totalOutstandingCents` as a dedicated `SUM` over the full filtered set — invariant across pages, never a page subtotal (**AC-3**).
- **`src/orders/list.js`, `src/customers/list.js`** — peer paginated endpoints proving the default is API-wide.
- **`db/migrations/20260721_add_receivables_index.sql`** — a partial index matching the receivables filter, serving both the page query and the grand-total aggregate (**AC-5**; query plan verified to use it, 6,000-order report responds in ~17ms, far under the 2s budget).
- **4 test files (12 tests)** covering AC-1…AC-5, all green.

### Gates
`typecheck`, `lint`, `test` (12/12), and `format` all passed; `check-baselines` self-skipped (greenfield); code-review and base-sync passed.

### Two environment quirks handled (logged as friction)
1. `.agents/` isn't committed in this sandbox, so it was absent from the worktree and the `check-baselines` gate's script couldn't resolve — fixed with a local (uncommitted) symlink to the main checkout's `.agents`.
2. `package.json` is on the harness's `.git/info/exclude` "framework overlay (never commit)" list, so it's intentionally not part of the delivered commit. The application is self-sufficient on Node built-ins, and `tsconfig.json`/`biome.json` (which *are* committed) tune the overlay's tooling to the code.

The final "arm-failure" block was a false negative (the local branch-delete step failed *after* the PR had already merged); the idempotent `single-story-confirm-merge.js` reconciled the stale label and ran the land tail.
